IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified 15 high voltage poles that need replacing and upgrading. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. Our contractors Avidety may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. During this time, you were connected to a generator to provide you with electricity whilst we carry out the works. To disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power temporarily. This work is taking place in and around North Elham in Folkstone and Hythe. A road closure will be in place to allow the work to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

Sent by Talie at 20:08 - Just to let you know that our Food van and Customer Support vehicles will be leaving the Barclay Road and Shernhall Street area for tonight shortly, however, they will be back tomorrow. The vans will remain on site until 21:00 to allow those who still need support the chance to visit before we leave.

Some of our Customer Champions are beginning to leave the area this evening, so you may notice less teams around, however some of the team will remain on site until 22:00 if you wanted to speak with them.

I appreciate that having to ready yourself for another night with no power must be worrying, and we're still doing everything we can to get the lights back on. We'll be here to keep you updated, we're here 24/7.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 17:27 - Just to let you know that our Support Services are still in the area and are ready to help in any way they can.

Our Food Van is still located outside 180 Shernhall Street, at the junction of Maynard Road and our Customer Support Vehicles are also still located both outside 84 Barclay Road and opposite the Raglan Pub on Shernhall Street. They'll both be on site until 21:00 tonight, so please feel free to see them. 

We'll be here to keep you updated, and if you have any questions our colleagues are always here to talk over the phone.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Roland at 04:36 - Thank you for your patience while our engineers have diverted power around the fault on the underground network in the Rochester ME2 area. We have now restored power to all but one property. We're sorry for any disruption this unexpected power cut caused.

If your power isn't back on, we'd still like to keep you updated. If we aren't currently in contact with you by phone or in person at your property, please call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Rochester ME2 area and are investigating the fault. You may find your power is going on and off; that is normal for this type of issue and is due to our engineers working to divert power remotely. By doing this, we've managed to restore power to 7 customers. There are now 19 properties still affected. We'll keep sending SMS updates until everybody is restored. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Sent by Roland at 03:51.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
